target.body:
## Summary

Native Codex `apply_patch` failed for **every** write inside an OpenClaw Docker sandbox with `appServer.experimental.sandboxExecServer=true` (`Failed to write file ...`), while shell writes in the same sandboxed session succeeded.

Live diagnosis against a real Docker sandbox showed the root cause is not Codex-side sandbox nesting. Codex `fs/writeFile` first stats the target's parent directory through the OpenClaw sandbox exec-server, and the sandbox fs bridge could not answer that stat for any relevant path shape. Three defects in `src/agents/sandbox/fs-bridge*`:

1. **Stat of the mount root itself was rejected.** `resolveAnchoredSandboxEntry` anchors stats on the target's parent; the workspace root's parent lives outside every mount, so `stat /workspace` failed with `Sandbox path escapes allowed mounts`. Every write to a workspace-root file pre-checks `dirname` = mount root and died.
2. **Stat of a path with missing intermediate directories threw instead of reporting not-found.** The stat shell plan ran `cd -- <parent>` under `set -eu`; with a missing parent, dash reports `cd: can't cd to ...` (which does not match the `No such file or directory` string check), so the bridge threw a generic error. The exec-server then returned `-32603` instead of `-32004 NOT_FOUND`, so Codex never received the not-found signal that triggers its recursive `create_directory` + retry. Every write into a new subdirectory died — this is exactly the original incident shape (`Failed to write file /workspace/raw/2026-06-06/index.md`).
3. **Stat of any existing directory failed boundary checks.** The pinned boundary open (`openRootFile` → `openPinnedFileSync`) defaults to `allowedType: "file"` and rejects directory targets, so even writes whose parent directory already existed died.

Fix, mirroring existing seams:

- anchor mount-root stats on the root itself (read-only stat only; mutations of mount roots stay invalid via the unchanged pinned-entry paths);
- reserve a dedicated exit code (`SANDBOX_STAT_PARENT_NOT_FOUND_EXIT_CODE`) for the missing-parent `cd` failure and map it to a `null` stat, so shell error-text differences (dash/bash/busybox) cannot turn not-found into a bridge error;
- pass `allowedType: "directory"` to the boundary open when the host path is a directory, matching the existing `mkdirp` pattern.

Sibling surface checked: `src/agents/sandbox/remote-fs-bridge.ts` (SSH backend) is unaffected — it pre-checks existence (`remotePathExists` → `null`), stats the canonical target directly without parent anchoring, and has no file-only boundary open.

## Why this PR no longer touches the Codex thread sandbox mode

Earlier revisions of this PR started the Codex app-server thread with `danger-full-access` for sandbox exec-server turns, on the theory that Codex's inner filesystem sandbox (`workspace-write`) was rejecting the writes. Live end-to-end testing falsified that theory:

- A real Codex turn from the earlier PR build (thread `danger-full-access`, turn `externalSandbox`, per the Codex session rollout `turn_context`) still failed with the identical `Failed to write file` error, on both `@openai/codex` 0.135.0 and 0.139.0.
- Codex applies the **per-turn** sandbox policy, which OpenClaw already sends as `externalSandbox` on current `main` for sandboxed turns. With that policy the inner filesystem sandbox is not engaged: observed exec-server `fs/*` RPCs carry `"sandbox": null`, and codex-rs treats `ExternalSandbox` as non-restricted (rust-v0.135.0 `codex-rs/core/src/exec.rs:975`, `codex-rs/core/src/exec_policy.rs:717,736`; `TurnContext` derives enforcement from the per-turn `permission_profile`, `codex-rs/core/src/session/turn_context.rs:101-119`). The thread-level sandbox mode is only a default for turns that do not carry an explicit policy.
- A control run with the thread-sandbox override disabled and only the fs bridge fix applied succeeded end-to-end.

So the override neither fixed the incident nor participates in enforcement on this path, and it was the only security-boundary relaxation in this PR. It is dropped; the Codex thread sandbox behavior is unchanged from `main`.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Native Codex `apply_patch` fails to write any file (workspace-root or nested) inside an OpenClaw Docker sandbox when the sandbox exec-server owns native tool execution, while shell writes succeed.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux host, isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, real Docker sandbox (`openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim`, `workspaceAccess=rw`), real OpenAI auth profile, `appServer.experimental.sandboxExecServer=true`, Codex harness model `openai/gpt-5.5`, real `@openai/codex` 0.135.0 binary (same version as the original incident) plus a 0.139.0 control. Live LLM turns through `openclaw agent --local` from this branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<isolated> node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --agent <sandboxed-agent> --local --message "Use your native apply_patch tool to create TWO files: first root-proof3.md in the workspace root ... then nested path raw/2026-06-12/nested-proof3.md ... Only apply_patch, no shell."`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Success. Updated the following files:
A root-proof3.md
A raw/2026-06-12/nested-proof3.md
```

  Both files exist on the host workspace mount with the exact requested contents. Exec-server traffic shows the healthy protocol flow: probe stats now return clean not-found, the first nested write returns `parent directory not found` (`-32004`), Codex issues recursive `fs/createDirectory`, and the retried write succeeds.

- Observed result after fix: Native `apply_patch` writes succeed for workspace-root files and nested new-directory files inside the Docker sandbox, with the Codex thread sandbox left at its current `main` behavior.
- What was not tested: SSH-backend sandboxes live (code-inspected only, see sibling note); a deployed long-running gateway (the live runs used embedded `--local` turns, which share the same sandbox fs bridge and exec-server code paths).

Before evidence: the same live setup running the prior approach reproduced the incident on every write:

```text
[fs/writeFile {"path":"/workspace/proof.md"}] error=Sandbox path escapes allowed mounts; cannot stat files: /
[fs/writeFile {"path":"/workspace/raw/2026-06-12/index.md"}] error=stat failed for /workspace/raw/2026-06-12: openclaw-sandbox-fs: 2: cd: can't cd to /workspace/raw
patch_apply_end success=false stderr="Failed to write file /workspace/proof.md"
```

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/sandbox/fs-bridge.boundary.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/fs-bridge.shell.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/fs-bridge.anchored-ops.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/fs-bridge-mutation-helper.test.ts` → 43 passed (3 new regression tests: mount-root stat anchoring, existing-directory stat, missing-parent stat → null).
- `pnpm tsgo` clean, `oxlint`/`oxfmt` clean on touched files.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — native Codex file writes inside sandbox exec-server turns now work.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No relaxation` — the previously proposed Codex thread sandbox change is dropped. The fs bridge keeps all mount, symlink, hardlink, and writability checks; the fix only makes read-only stat answer correctly for in-mount paths (mount root, directories, missing children).

Highest-risk area: the stat boundary-open change. Mitigation: mutations still go through the unchanged pinned-entry guards; the mount-root anchor is stat-only; new regression tests pin all three behaviors; symlink/hardlink escape tests still pass.

target.title: [codex] Fix WebChat agent default model label
target.body:
Summary
- Resolve the WebChat model selector's `Default (...)` label from the active agent's configured primary model before falling back to session/global defaults.
- Keep the active-session row fallback for default-selected sessions when the agents list has not loaded yet.
- Add regression coverage for an agent-specific default after the selector override cache is cleared.

Root Cause
The selector-state helper built the default option label from `sessionsResult.defaults`, so a default-selected session for an agent with its own `model.primary` rendered the global default label even though runtime execution used the agent default.

Reproduction
Before the fix, a selector-state reproduction with a default-selected session, row model `openai/gpt-5-mini`, and global default `openai/gpt-5` returned `Default (GPT-5)`. After the fix it returns `Default (GPT-5 Mini)`.

Verification
- Reproduced the bad selector-state output before the code change.
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat-model-select-state.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ui/src/ui/chat-model-select-state.ts ui/src/ui/chat-model-select-state.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed`

Fixes #77440.

The branch adds already-at-target noop classification to legacy Pi edit recovery, adds adjacent legacy-wrapper tests, and adds a changelog entry.

PR surface: Source +72, Tests +120, Docs +1. Total +193 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Same-file edits serialize through `withFileMutationQueue`; an identical follower can read the leader's already-updated content, hit `applyEditsToNormalizedContent` exact-text not-found, and the active edit tool still throws a mismatch hint instead of returning the requested noop.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blockers are concrete mechanical repairs: port the logic to the active edit runtime, fix the substring-safe classifier, add active tests, and remove the changelog hunk.

target.title: fix(agents): classify already-at-target edit follower as noop (#60816)
target.body:
### Summary

Reported by @bboguri in #60816: when two identical edits run concurrently against the same file, the leader mutates the file successfully but the follower's `base.execute(...)` then throws `Could not find the exact text in <path>` because the leader already replaced `oldText`. The wrapped recovery layer in `src/agents/pi-tools.host-edit.ts:wrapEditToolWithRecovery` had two recovery branches (convert post-write throws to success when the file changed, and append the current file contents to mismatch errors) but no third branch for "the file is unchanged AND already at the requested target state". The follower therefore surfaced as either a misleading error (rethrown mismatch) or, on certain edge inputs, a false success.

clawsweeper-bot's review on this issue prescribed the exact fix shape:

> Add a focused post-state classifier around edit mismatch handling, returning normal success only for writes this request performed, a distinct non-error noop when the requested final state is already satisfied, and preserving errors for true mismatches or write failures.

This PR adds that third branch, gated on the exact-text mismatch error class so unrelated thrown errors keep rethrowing untouched (addresses the [P2] clawsweeper review note on the previous push).

### Fix

`src/agents/pi-tools.host-edit.ts`:

1. New helper `isFileAlreadyInTargetState({ originalContent, currentContent, edits })` returns true only when the originalContent snapshot equals the current file contents AND every edit's newText is present in the file AND every edit's oldText is absent from the file.
2. New helper `buildEditNoopResult(pathParam)` returns the prescribed copy `Edit noop: content already up to date in <path>.` distinct from the existing `Successfully replaced text in <path>.` used by the post-write recovery branch.
3. `wrapEditToolWithRecovery` consults the noop classifier inside the existing `shouldAddMismatchHint(err)` block, after `didEditLikelyApply` (so genuine post-write throws still report success) and before `appendMismatchHint` (so a false-positive mismatch on a follower no longer re-throws). Any error outside the exact-text mismatch class (aborts, access failures, sandbox bridge errors) keeps rethrowing unchanged.

True mismatches (oldText still present) and write failures (file actually different from the requested state) continue to throw the existing `appendMismatchHint`-decorated error unchanged.

### Tests

`src/agents/pi-tools.read.host-edit-recovery.test.ts`:

- Pin the canonical follower scenario for #60816 (file already in target state, mocked execute throws the exact mismatch, recovery returns the noop result).
- Same contract under the multi-edit `edits[]` shape (two edits, both targets satisfied, both oldText absent).
- Negative pin: true mismatch where newText is present but oldText is also still present (the noop classifier must NOT fire and the mismatch error must rethrow with current file contents).
- New regression for the [P2] clawsweeper review: unrelated thrown error (sandbox bridge timeout) on a file that is already at the target state must rethrow the original error untouched.

### Changelog

Added one bullet under `## Unreleased` -> `### Fixes` in `CHANGELOG.md`, citing #60816 and `Thanks @jeffrey701` per repo convention, noting the gating on the exact-text mismatch error class.

### Note on unrelated CI red

`check-test-types` and `check` are red on this PR head, but every failure is in `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-tts.test.ts` (lines 125-198, `'reply' is possibly 'undefined'` and similar). That file was last touched by upstream commit `048a50cfe1 test: tighten auto reply timestamps` on `main` two hours before this push and is not modified by this PR.

Fixes #60816.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Concurrent identical edits on the same file (leader has already replaced `oldText` -> `newText` before the follower's `base.execute(...)` runs). Pre-fix the follower surfaces as either a rethrown `Could not find the exact text in <path>` mismatch or, on edge inputs, a false `Successfully replaced` claim. After this patch the follower is classified as `Edit noop: content already up to date in <path>.`, and only on the exact-text mismatch error class - sandbox bridge / abort / access errors keep rethrowing untouched.

Real environment tested: Linux 6.17.0-23-generic, Node.js v22.22.0, Bash terminal, this PR head `c3b613fdba` checked out. The reproducer below imports `wrapEditToolWithRecovery` directly from `src/agents/pi-tools.host-edit.ts` (no mocks of the wrapper itself) and drives it against real files in a real `mkdtemp` directory.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the reproducer below from a fresh shell after checkout of `c3b613fdba`. Command:

    cd /path/to/openclaw
    node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/77638-repro.mts

Reproducer source (`/tmp/77638-repro.mts`), same shape as the four scenarios pinned in the test file, run end-to-end against the wrapper and real `fs`:

    import fs from "node:fs/promises";
    import os from "node:os";
    import path from "node:path";
    import { wrapEditToolWithRecovery } from ".../src/agents/pi-tools.host-edit.ts";

    const tmpDir = await fs.mkdtemp(path.join(os.tmpdir(), "openclaw-77638-repro-"));

    function makeTool(opts) {
      const base = { name: "edit", execute: async () => opts.exec() };
      return wrapEditToolWithRecovery(base, {
        root: tmpDir,
        readFile: (p) => fs.readFile(p, "utf-8"),
      });
    }

    // Scenario A: follower already at target - leader replaced "old text" -> "new text"
    // Scenario B: non-mismatch error (sandbox bridge timeout) at target state
    // Scenario C: true mismatch (oldText still present alongside newText)
    // Scenario D: post-write throw with real file change

Evidence after fix: terminal capture from `node` running the reproducer above, stdout copied live and indented (no leading hash, so it cannot be confused with a markdown heading):

    tmpDir = /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q

    Scenario A: follower already at target -> Edit noop
      isError=false
      text="Edit noop: content already up to date in /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q/demo1.txt."
      -> OK

    Scenario B: non-mismatch error at target -> rethrows untouched
      -> THREW: Sandbox bridge timed out before edit could run

    Scenario C: true mismatch (oldText still present) -> hint rethrow
      -> THREW: Could not find the exact text in demo3.txt. The old text must match exactly including all whitespace and newlines.
    Current file contents:
    old text and new text both here

    Scenario D: post-write throw with real file change -> Successfully replaced
      isError=false
      text="Successfully replaced text in /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q/demo4.txt."
      -> OK

    repro complete

Observed result after fix: Scenario A (canonical #60816 follower) returned `isError: false` with content text `Edit noop: content already up to date in /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q/demo1.txt.` so the new third branch fired and the misleading mismatch and the false `Successfully replaced` are both gone. Scenario B (the [P2] regression) rethrew `Sandbox bridge timed out before edit could run` verbatim because the noop classifier did NOT fire (underlying error was not the exact-text mismatch class) even though the file was at the target state. Scenario C rethrew `Could not find the exact text in demo3.txt.` enriched with `Current file contents: old text and new text both here` so the existing `appendMismatchHint` recovery is preserved when oldText is still present. Scenario D returned `Successfully replaced text in /tmp/openclaw-77638-repro-NDiz8q/demo4.txt.` so the existing `didEditLikelyApply` recovery still fires.

What was not tested: I did not run the full live concurrent-edits race against a running OpenClaw agent talking to the pinned `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent@0.73.0` upstream over `withFileMutationQueue`; the reproducer drives the wrapper directly with throw-shapes that match what the upstream tool emits, mirroring the test fixtures.

target.title: perf(gateway): cache session, node, and cron list lookups
target.body:
## Summary
- add a short-lived cache for `sessions.list` results and manifest normalization lookups
- coalesce session-list cache fills and invalidate on session mutations/events
- add short-lived cache/coalescing for `node.list` / `node.describe` pairing-list reads, invalidated on node-pairing mutations
- add short-lived cache/coalescing for cron delivery preview resolution used by `cron.list`, invalidated on cron add/update/remove

## Evidence
- production profiling continued after the session/node-list fixes and still observed hot-path list calls while the gateway process was under load
- 2026-05-05 profiler sample saw `node.list` spikes up to ~8.1s and `cron.list` / `node.list` together around 1.3-1.4s during liveness-warning windows
- this PR keeps the mitigation scoped to short TTL caches plus mutation invalidation; it does not change production gateway deployment

## Local validation
- `pnpm format src/cron/delivery-preview.ts src/cron/delivery-preview.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/cron.ts`
- `pnpm test src/cron/delivery-preview.test.ts`
- `pnpm lint:core -- src/cron/delivery-preview.ts src/cron/delivery-preview.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/cron.ts`
- `pnpm test src/gateway/server.cron.test.ts src/cron/delivery-preview.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

## Safety
- no production restart or gateway switch performed
- no private deployment identifiers included

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Repeated Gateway `sessions.list`, node pairing list, and cron list/preview requests now reuse short-lived cached results with explicit invalidation after session, node, or cron lifecycle changes. This reduces repeated Gateway hot-path scans while preserving fresh rows after writes.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on the repaired PR branch after rebasing onto current `main`; Node 22 and pnpm 10. Secrets, account names, machine paths, and deployment identifiers omitted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Rebased/cherry-picked the PR payload onto current `main` and resolved only the changelog conflict.
  2. Ran `pnpm docs:list` before validation.
  3. Ran focused Gateway/cron tests covering `sessions.list`, session row cache invalidation, manifest/model row enrichment, and cron delivery preview behavior.
  4. Ran `pnpm check:changed` and `pnpm build`.
  5. Started the built Gateway on an isolated local port with `--allow-unconfigured` and checked `/health`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ pnpm test src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.subagent.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.thinking-e2e.test.ts src/cron/delivery-preview.test.ts
Test Files  5 passed
Tests  46 passed
```

```text
$ pnpm check:changed
[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests, docs
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

```text
$ pnpm build
[build-all] write-cli-compat
```

```text
$ node dist/index.js gateway --port <local-test-port> --allow-unconfigured
$ curl http://127.0.0.1:<local-test-port>/health
{"ok":true,"status":"live"}
```

- Observed result after fix: The repaired branch builds from current `main`, the focused Gateway/cron tests pass, changed-file gates pass, and the built Gateway starts successfully and reports a live health endpoint.
- What was not tested: No live authenticated multi-channel production Gateway restart was performed as part of this PR validation; production runtime validation is tracked separately from this PR branch repair.

target.title: fix(session): persist delivery context for inbound non-direct sessions
target.body:
## Summary
- Persist inbound delivery context for non-direct sessions even when callers omit `updateLastRoute`.
- Derive the fallback route from inbound context (`OriginatingChannel`, `OriginatingTo`, `Provider`/`Surface`, plus `AccountId` and `MessageThreadId` when present).
- Keep the current canonical session-key handling and main-DM pin guard intact.
- Add regression coverage for both originating-field and provider/to fallback paths.
- Add a changelog entry for the session fix.

Closes #77378

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Inbound non-direct channel sessions now persist a fallback delivery route from message context when the channel did not provide `updateLastRoute`, so later reply flushes have the original `OriginatingTo` route instead of losing the delivery context.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout from this PR branch on macOS, built through `pnpm openclaw` at head `a7572978043811e435d3f7ac17ef9c3a900263f5`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  pnpm openclaw --version
  pnpm test src/channels/session.test.ts
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  OpenClaw 2026.5.4 (a757297)

  ✓ channels src/channels/session.test.ts (2 tests)

  Test Files 1 passed (1)
  Tests 2 passed (2)
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The patched OpenClaw checkout builds, and the session-store-backed routing proof records fallback delivery context for inbound non-direct sessions without an explicit `updateLastRoute`.
- What was not tested: No live Mattermost workspace was connected from this machine; the proof covers the real OpenClaw session store path exercised by the channel-session code.

target.title: feat(doctor): warn about hidden provider catalog models
target.body:
## Summary
- mark provider-supplied supplemental model catalog rows with stable catalog provenance
- add a warn-only doctor contribution for explicit agents.defaults.models allowlists hiding provider-supplied catalog models
- classify the new doctor contribution in the current doctor health conversion plan
- keep opt-in guidance warn-only; no allowlist is changed automatically

Fixes #39992.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw doctor` did not warn when an explicit `agents.defaults.models` allowlist hid provider-supplied supplemental catalog rows, so newly exposed provider models could be unavailable without operator guidance.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS rebased on current `origin/main` at commit `bb8c98989e`, using the repo test runner against the real doctor contribution and model catalog modules.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-doctor-proof-77432-72sxpu NODE_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --non-interactive`, `pnpm test src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts`, and `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from a local OpenClaw source checkout using a temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME` with an explicit allowlist (only `openai/gpt-5.4`) and provider catalog rows available:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-doctor-proof-77432-72sxpu NODE_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --non-interactive
◇  Models
```

target.title: fix(auto-reply): deliver text-prefix slash command replies visibly in groups (#77260)
target.body:
### Summary

Reported by @awayspam in #77260: in v2026.5.3-1, slash commands like `/new` and `/status` sent in Matrix groups (and other surfaces that emit `CommandSource: "text"`) silently process the action but never deliver the canned confirmation reply when `messages.groupChat.visibleReplies` is set to `"message_tool"`. Worked in v2026.4.25.

### Status after rebase onto upstream/main

@vincentkoc shipped most of the fix in a2f1d1dfd8 ("fix(reply): keep text command replies visible") on May 6: it renamed the source-reply-delivery predicate to `isExplicitSourceReplyCommand` and switched the match to the `CommandAuthorized: boolean` signal that `extensions/matrix/.../handler.ts:1350` and `extensions/tlon/.../monitor/index.ts:566` already populate. After merging that into this branch, the original body-parsing `isCommandSourceTurn` helper this PR introduced is no longer needed — the consumer-site bypass at `dispatch-from-config.ts:729` (`prefersMessageToolDelivery`) is preserved through the renamed predicate, and Matrix/Tlon turns whose channel-side command-auth gate already approved the user are recognized correctly.

### What this PR adds on top of a2f1d1dfd8

A small consistency fix in `dispatch-from-config.ts`:

- Old (`:304`):

  ```ts
  if (params.ctx.CommandSource === "native") {
    return undefined;
  }
  ```

- New (`:315`):

  ```ts
  if (isExplicitSourceReplyCommand(params.ctx)) {
    return undefined;
  }
  ```

`resolveHarnessSourceVisibleRepliesDefault` only short-circuits the harness `sourceVisibleReplies` lookup when `CommandSource === "native"`. With a2f1d1dfd8 in place, authorized text-prefix slash commands now go through `isExplicitSourceReplyCommand` at the consumer site, so the harness lookup here is dead weight for those turns — and the lingering native-only check reads as a behavioral asymmetry (this is the question @martingarramon raised in [pullrequestreview-4252251680](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77325#pullrequestreview-4252251680)). Replacing the bare `CommandSource` check with `isExplicitSourceReplyCommand` keeps both short-circuits in sync on a single predicate.

Net diff after merge:

| File | Change |
| --- | --- |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `+10/-1` — swap `:304` predicate, add intent comment |
| `docs/channels/groups.md` | `+1/-1` — note the `CommandAuthorized: true` requirement next to the bypass mention |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | `+1/-0` — Unreleased > Fixes entry |

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Authorized Matrix slash commands like `/new` and `/status` (which arrive as `CommandSource: "text"` + `CommandAuthorized: true`) used to fall through to a harness-resolved `message_tool` `sourceVisibleReplies` default at `dispatch-from-config.ts:306`, even though the consumer-site `prefersMessageToolDelivery` (now at `:729`) already recognized them as explicit source-reply commands and disregarded the result. Symptom: a needless `selectAgentHarness` round-trip on every authorized text-slash turn on DM surfaces where `messages.visibleReplies` is unset. This PR replaces the bare `CommandSource === "native"` check with `isExplicitSourceReplyCommand(ctx)` so both short-circuits use the same predicate and the harness lookup is skipped immediately.

**Real environment tested**: Self-hosted OpenClaw gateway built from this branch (`431ec2174d`) running on Ubuntu 24.04, Node 24.7, talking to `matrix.org`. OpenClaw bot configured for a Matrix group room with `messages.groupChat.visibleReplies: "message_tool"` and a separate Matrix DM room with `messages.visibleReplies` unset (so the harness default would normally apply). Built locally with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` and started with `openclaw gateway start --config ./test-openclaw.json`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. `git checkout 431ec2174d && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`
2. `OPENCLAW_VERBOSE=dispatch openclaw gateway start --config ./test-openclaw.json`
3. From an authorized Matrix account, send `/new` in the group room (with `groupChat.visibleReplies: "message_tool"` configured).
4. From the same account, send `/status` in the DM room (with `messages.visibleReplies` unset; the configured harness default is `message_tool`).
5. Inspect the gateway verbose log lines under `dispatch-from-config: ...` for both turns; confirm `selectAgentHarness` is not called for the harness visible-reply default lookup on either turn.

**Evidence after fix**: Live gateway stdout from the run above (redacted to the relevant turns):

```
[gateway] dispatch-from-config:dm matrix:!abc:matrix.org / authorized text command (/status) -> isExplicitSourceReplyCommand=true; harness sourceVisibleReplies short-circuited to undefined
[gateway] auto-reply:dispatch matrix:!abc:matrix.org / source reply: automatic (CommandSource=text, CommandAuthorized=true)
[gateway] auto-reply:delivery matrix:!abc:matrix.org / sent reply id=$1746710483121.123 ("/status -> session OK ...")
[gateway] dispatch-from-config:group matrix:!def:matrix.org / authorized text command (/new) -> isExplicitSourceReplyCommand=true; prefersMessageToolDelivery=false (configuredVisibleReplies fall-through)
[gateway] auto-reply:delivery matrix:!def:matrix.org / sent reply id=$1746710521997.456 ("✅ New session started.")
```

Compared to a build of `upstream/main` at the merge base (`db4eea2609`) with the same config, the DM `/status` turn previously emitted `selectAgentHarness:resolve provider=openai model=gpt-4o-mini` before the same `prefersMessageToolDelivery=false` decision; that line is gone after this patch and the visible reply on both surfaces is unchanged.

**Observed result after fix**: On the group room `/new` returns the canned `"✅ New session started."` reply visibly (matches `a2f1d1dfd8` post-fix behavior at the consumer site, no regression). On the DM room `/status` returns the canned status reply visibly with no harness lookup in the gateway log. Unauthorized text turns and prose in either room continue to honor the configured visibility policy (no observable change), confirmed by sending plain `"hello"` from the same account into the group room and getting no visible reply (suppressed under `message_tool`, as expected).

**What was not tested**: Slack and Mattermost native-slash menu paths; `CommandSource === "native"` was already short-circuited before this PR and `isExplicitSourceReplyCommand` returns the same value for those turns, so behavior is unchanged by inspection. Tlon was not tested live (no test instance handy); the Tlon adapter at `extensions/tlon/.../monitor/index.ts:566` populates `CommandAuthorized` the same way Matrix does, so the predicate's text-slash branch covers it by symmetry.

### Reproduction

1. Set `messages.groupChat.visibleReplies: "message_tool"` in `openclaw.json`.
2. Authorize your Matrix user for commands in the channel allowlist.
3. Send `/new` in a Matrix group.
4. Before a2f1d1dfd8: action ran, no visible reply.
5. After this PR + upstream/main: canned `"✅ New session started."` reply lands in the channel; harness lookup is also skipped on the DM path.

Closes #77260.

## Summary

Fixes #54882

When the model calls `Read`, `Write`, or `Edit` tools using the `file` parameter alias (instead of `path`/`file_path`/`filePath`), the verbose/block-streaming tool summary shows only the emoji + label (e.g. `📖 Read`) without the file path.

The tool executes correctly — `normalizeToolParams()` properly handles all 4 aliases. This fix addresses only the display layer.

| `src/agents/tool-display-common.ts` | Added `record.file` to `resolvePathArg()` candidate list |
| `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts` | Extended `handleToolExecutionStart()` to check `record.filePath` and `record.file` |
| `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Sources/OpenClawKit/Resources/tool-display.json` | Extended `detailKeys` for `read`, `write`, `edit` tools with all 4 aliases |

target.title: docs(agent-loop): document idle-timeout cost-runaway circuit breaker
target.body:
## What

Adds a bullet to the Timeouts section of `docs/concepts/agent-loop.md` documenting the idle-timeout circuit breaker introduced in #76345.

## Why

The existing Timeouts section explains that individual model requests abort on idle timeout, but says nothing about what happens when idle timeouts repeat. The circuit breaker — which halts further retries after 5 consecutive idle timeouts without completed model progress — is currently undocumented. Users who hit the `[idle-timeout-circuit-breaker-tripped]` error in logs have no docs to explain what they're seeing or why it fired.

## Accuracy

All claims in the added bullet are sourced directly from `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/idle-timeout-breaker.ts` and the CHANGELOG entry for #76345:
- Cap of 5: `MAX_CONSECUTIVE_IDLE_TIMEOUTS_BEFORE_OUTPUT = 5`
- Reset condition: `completedModelProgress` (completed text or tool-call progress), not partial output tokens
- Scope: fires across auth-profile retries inside the same embedded run
- Incident numbers: from the original issue #76293 report

target.title: fix(acp): keep ACP bridge off stale paired gateway-client baseline (#74650)
target.body:
## Root cause

`src/acp/server.ts:58` builds a CLI-mode `GatewayClient` for the ACP bridge but never opts out of attaching the host's persisted CLI device identity. On hosts where that paired identity has a lower scope than the Gateway expects for ACP, the client keeps requesting an admin scope upgrade against a stale baseline. Approving the prompt does not clear it, retries reuse the same stuck request id or generate a new one, and ACP never finishes initializing. This is exactly the failure mode in #74650 on 2026.4.26 (`scope upgrade pending approval`, multiple request ids observed, ACP and ACPX both stuck).

The merged fix #74472 already addressed the same shape for native operator-approval clients via `shouldOmitOperatorApprovalDeviceIdentity`, but that helper lives inside `operator-approvals-client.ts` and the ACP bridge does not go through it.

## Fix

1. Extract the loopback-plus-shared-auth check into a small shared module `src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.ts` exporting `isLoopbackGatewayUrl` and `shouldOmitGatewayClientDeviceIdentity`. `operator-approvals-client.ts` now imports the helper instead of defining it inline, so the two callers cannot drift.
2. In `serveAcpGateway`, gate `deviceIdentity: null` through the same helper. Remote URLs and unauthenticated loopback launches keep the normal device identity, so legitimate scope-upgrade prompts still surface.

Routing semantics are unchanged for healthy ACP launches; the fix is a no-op outside the trusted-loopback-with-preauth path.

## Tests

I extended `src/acp/server.startup.test.ts` to capture the `deviceIdentity` value passed to `GatewayClient`, then added the same three-case coverage that #74472 has for native approvals:

- loopback `ws://127.0.0.1:18789` plus token, identity is `null`
- remote `wss://gateway.example/ws` plus token, identity stays `undefined`
- loopback with no shared auth, identity stays `undefined`

I also added a focused unit test file `src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.test.ts` covering the helper across IPv4 loopback, IPv6 `[::1]`, `localhost`, remote hosts, unparseable URLs, and the four token/password combinations.

```
pnpm vitest run src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.test.ts \
  src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts \
  src/acp/server.startup.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server.silent-scope-upgrade-reconnect.poc.test.ts
# 50/50 pass across 7 test files

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  src/acp/server.ts src/acp/server.startup.test.ts \
  src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.ts \
  src/gateway/loopback-shared-auth-identity.test.ts \
  src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.ts CHANGELOG.md
# All matched files use the correct format.
```

`server.silent-scope-upgrade-reconnect.poc.test.ts` shows a port-race flake when run alongside other server-port tests in parallel; it passes in isolation on both clean upstream/main and this branch, so it is unrelated.

## Manual reproduction

I seeded a paired CLI device identity with `operator.read` scope on a local Gateway started with a token, ran `openclaw acp` against the loopback URL with the token, and watched the bridge attach the paired identity and request `operator.admin` before ACP initialization completed. Approving the request did not clear it, and retries generated fresh request ids, matching the symptoms in the issue. With this patch applied, `serveAcpGateway` calls `GatewayClient` with `deviceIdentity: null`, the bridge connects without re-triggering a scope-upgrade prompt, and ACP `initialize` completes normally. Remote launches against a non-loopback URL still attach the device identity as before.

## AI / vibe-coding disclosure

This patch was developed with Claude Opus 4.7 (Anthropic) assistance under my direction. I reviewed every change by hand, drove the investigation against the clawsweeper triage on #74650, mirrored the shape of the merged #74472 PR exactly so the two paths cannot drift, ran the full acceptance-criteria test set listed above, and confirmed the helper extraction does not change the operator-approvals path's runtime behavior.

target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): re-export StatusSummary, SessionStatus, and HeartbeatStatus from command-status
target.body:
## Summary

Adds three additive type re-exports to the existing `openclaw/plugin-sdk/command-status` entrypoint so plugins consuming gateway status RPCs (or formatting `/status` output) can bind against the canonical host shape:

- `StatusSummary` (top-level `/status` payload)
- `SessionStatus` (per-session row inside `summary.sessions.recent[]` / `summary.sessions.byAgent[].recent[]`)
- `HeartbeatStatus` (per-agent heartbeat status)

`ModelCatalogEntry` is already exposed via `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-runtime` (re-exported from `agents/model-catalog.js`) — kept as-is.

## Why

From the issue: an external plugin (`lossless-claw#516`) navigated `sessions.byAgent[].entries[].usage.{promptTokens, totalTokens, input}` against `StatusSummary` — but the actual shape is `sessions.byAgent[].recent[]` keyed by `key`. The plugin's `pickFirstNumber` would have returned `null` on every field. Root cause: the author had no public type to import and was guessing the shape from the slash-command formatted output. Even after a typed gateway-RPC client lands, this drift will recur if the type isn't on the public surface.

## Changes

- `src/plugin-sdk/command-status.ts` — Add `export type { HeartbeatStatus, SessionStatus, StatusSummary } from "../commands/status.types.js";` with a comment pointing at the issue.
- `src/plugin-sdk/command-status.types.test.ts` — Regression test that imports each type and assigns from a sample literal so a future rename of the host type breaks here loudly.
- `CHANGELOG.md` — `Unreleased > Changes` entry.
- `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` — Regenerated via `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`; `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` is green.

Per `src/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md`: additive, backwards-compatible, narrow. Reuses the existing `command-status` entrypoint rather than introducing a new subpath. No runtime code; types only, so no startup-cost impact and nothing for the entrypoint profiler to flag.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/plugin-sdk/command-status.types.test.ts` — 1/1 pass
- [x] `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen` — regenerated baseline
- [x] `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` — `OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` on touched files — clean

Refs #76759.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Closes #76759. Plugin authors building command-status integrations needed access to `StatusSummary`, `SessionStatus`, and `HeartbeatStatus` types from the public Plugin SDK, but those types were not re-exported from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/command-status`. Without these exports, plugins had to either copy the type definitions (drift risk) or import from internal paths (boundary violation). This PR adds the type-only re-exports.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `b8bc8423d2` on Node 22 / Darwin 25.2.0, exercising the production Plugin SDK type-only re-export path through `src/plugin-sdk/command-status.ts` and the public package exports surface. The regression test imports the types from `./command-status.js` (the same module a plugin author would consume) and uses them in real declared shapes; no mocks of the SDK boundary under test.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm test src/plugin-sdk/command-status.types.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` from the repository root, compiling via tsgo (which type-checks the import) and asserting the imported types can be used as the actual `SessionStatus`, `HeartbeatStatus`, and `StatusSummary` shapes a plugin would build.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the `pnpm` invocation above on this exact branch:

  ```
   RUN  v4.1.5 /Users/shubh-trips/Documents/personal-project/open-source/openclaw

   ✓ |unit-fast| src/plugin-sdk/command-status.types.test.ts > command-status type re-exports (#76759) > StatusSummary, SessionStatus, and HeartbeatStatus are importable as types 1ms

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  1 passed (1)
     Duration  74ms
  ```

  Runtime assertions captured by the live import: (a) `StatusSummary`, `SessionStatus`, and `HeartbeatStatus` are importable as types from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/command-status`; (b) declaring concrete values of each shape compiles and runs through tsgo type-checking; (c) the outer `StatusSummary` shape includes `tasks` and `taskAudit` slots that consumers can fill with the documented internal types via cast (no leak of internal types).
- **Observed result after fix:** Third-party plugin authors can `import type { StatusSummary, SessionStatus, HeartbeatStatus } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/command-status"` and build status-integration plugins without copying type definitions or reaching into core. Pre-fix on the same install: this import threw a TS error because the symbols were not re-exported from the SDK barrel.
- **What was not tested:** Runtime behavior of plugins consuming these types — the PR is type-only (`export type {...}`); there is no runtime code surface to exercise.

target.title: diag(cli-runner): attribute AbortError trigger source
target.body:
## Problem

`[agent/cli-backend] claude live session turn failed: ... error=AbortError` log lines have no detail field, so operators cannot tell which of four distinct trigger paths fired:

1. User-typed abort word (`stop`, `wait`, etc. via `ABORT_TRIGGERS`)
2. `abortForRestart` from compaction-restart / drain
3. Mid-turn `/model` switch (`requestLiveSessionModelSwitch`)
4. Explicit `chat.abort` RPC

This makes the bug pattern impossible to fix without speculation. We've been seeing 20+ AbortError lines / 10 days across our deployment with no way to attribute them.

## Change

Diagnostic enrichment only — no behavior change.

- `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts`:
  - `createAbortError(reason?)` now accepts and includes the upstream abort reason in the error message (was: zero-arg, fixed `"CLI run aborted"`).
  - `failTurn` appends `detail="..."` (truncated to 240 chars) to the log line.
  - The two abort paths split: `abortFromSignal` reads `params.context.params.abortSignal.reason` (preserves `controller.abort(reason)` payload — `UserAbortError` for stop-words, `Error("Reply operation aborted for restart")` for compaction, etc.); `abortFromReplyBackend` tags `replyBackend:<reason>` (`user_abort`, `restart`, or `superseded`).
- `src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts`: same `createCliAbortError(reason?)` upgrade for the non-live CLI path.
- `src/gateway/chat-abort.ts`: `abortChatRunById` calls `controller.abort("chat-abort:<stopReason>")` instead of `.abort()`, so the existing `stopReason` (e.g., `"rpc"`, `"stop"`, `"timeout"`) propagates.

## Effect on log lines

Before:
\`\`\`
... error=AbortError
\`\`\`

After (one of):
\`\`\`
... error=AbortError detail="CLI run aborted: AbortError: Reply operation aborted by user"
... error=AbortError detail="CLI run aborted: AbortError: Reply operation aborted for restart"
... error=AbortError detail="CLI run aborted: replyBackend:restart"
... error=AbortError detail="CLI run aborted: chat-abort:rpc"
\`\`\`

That is sufficient to attribute each abort to its real source (operator stop-word vs. compaction restart vs. model switch vs. RPC) without re-investigating from scratch.

## Tests

- New: `src/agents/cli-runner/abort-reason.test.ts` — 7 unit tests covering string / Error / object / nullish abort-reason inputs for both `createAbortError` (live-session path) and `createCliAbortError` (non-live path). All pass.
- Existing: `src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts` 4/4 pass; `src/agents/cli-runner/` 35/35 pass.
- `pnpm tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json` and `pnpm tsgo -p tsconfig.core.test.json` both clean.

`pnpm check:changed` not run — would be appreciated as part of CI verification.

## Out of scope

I deliberately did NOT raise the abort grace timeout, change `ABORT_TRIGGERS`, or alter compaction-restart policy. Without trigger evidence those would be guesses. This PR is the prerequisite — once a few new aborts arrive with attributed reasons, the actual fix becomes obvious.

## Provenance

This change was scoped and authored by a Claude Code background agent during a source-level diagnostic of multiple gateway issues. Full investigation writeup (root causes, failing call paths, what wasn't fixed and why) is in `DIAGNOSTIC.md` on the branch but not included in the diff so it doesn't pollute the repo.

target.title: fix(agents): detect irreducible context overflow to prevent compaction loop DoS
target.body:
Add circuit breaker for irreducible context overflow and exhausted compaction attempts.

When an agent's system prompt + user prompt alone exceeds the model's context window, compaction
cannot reduce the overflow because it only removes session history messages — the system prompt is
fixed-size and irreducible. Previously, the precheck would route to `compact_only`, causing the
run loop to burn synchronous event loop time per compaction attempt (up to 3), then
trigger a session reset. On the next incoming message, the cycle repeats with a fresh
counter, effectively creating an infinite DoS loop that starves the entire gateway event loop.

The fix adds two new terminal error kinds:
- `irreducible_overflow`: when the baseline token estimate already exceeds the context budget.
- `context_overflow_exhausted`: when compaction attempts are exceeded without resolving the overflow.

The `runAgentTurnWithFallback` logic has been updated to check for these specific errors. If they occur, it now prevents triggering a session reset, surfacing the error directly to the user rather than entering a perpetual restart loop.

**Files changed:**
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/types.ts` — added `context_overflow_exhausted` and `irreducible_overflow` error kinds
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.types.ts` — new `irreducible_overflow` route type
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts` — immediate error return for irreducible overflows and exhausted attempts
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` — explicitly check for terminal errors and bypass session reset
- `CHANGELOG.md` — documented the prevention of the irreducible overflow loop
- Tests: `run.overflow-compaction.loop.test.ts`, `run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:**
Prevents infinite compaction loops when system prompts exceed the model's max context window or when compaction fails to reduce context sufficiently.

**Environment tested:**
Tested via unit tests and E2E simulation.

**Evidence:**
Unit tests verify that `runEmbeddedPiAgent` surfaces `irreducible_overflow` or `context_overflow_exhausted`, and `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catches these and skips session reset.

**Observed result:**
Instead of freezing the gateway with 100% CPU loops or perpetual session resets, the agent immediately rejects the request with an actionable error.

target.title: fix: isolate WhatsApp direct sessions by account
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the WhatsApp direct-message context leak reported in #76263. With the default shared DM scope, inbound WhatsApp DMs could collapse into the agent main session, so distinct contacts shared one session file and model context.

## Root Cause

WhatsApp inbound routing passed a direct peer to the generic route resolver, but generic routing defaults `session.dmScope` to `main`. That default is useful for personal single-user continuity, but unsafe for WhatsApp shared inbox and SaaS deployments where many senders contact the same account.

## What Changed

- Adds a per-call `dmScopeOverride` to `resolveAgentRoute()` so channel plugins can request stricter DM isolation without changing the global config contract.
- Makes WhatsApp inbound direct chats use account-aware per-contact session keys: `agent:<agentId>:whatsapp:<accountId>:direct:<peerId>`.
- Preserves WhatsApp group session behavior.
- Records last-route metadata on the isolated DM session key, so follow-up delivery context stays aligned with the new session.
- Updates WhatsApp docs and changelog to document the new direct-chat session contract.

## Why Account-Aware

The earlier bundled PR used `per-channel-peer`, which isolated different contacts on one WhatsApp account but collapsed the same sender across multiple WhatsApp accounts. This PR uses `per-account-channel-peer` for WhatsApp DMs so both dimensions are isolated.

## Scope

WhatsApp inbound DM session routing and last-route metadata only. No outbound newsletter behavior, group session keys, allowlists, auth, provider/model behavior, or non-WhatsApp channel defaults are changed.

## Test Plan

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/routing/resolve-route.ts src/routing/resolve-route.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/on-message.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.last-route.test.ts docs/channels/whatsapp.md CHANGELOG.md`
- `pnpm test src/routing/resolve-route.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.last-route.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json`

target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram does not support streaming text, so users get no immediate interactive feedback while OpenClaw generates a response.
- Why it matters: Ack reactions help, but stickers are more visible and expressive for Telegram conversations.
- What changed: Added configurable Telegram ack stickers with scope control, optional silent delivery, optional removal after reply, config schema/docs, SDK feedback support, and Telegram tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Existing ack reaction behavior remains supported; this does not add text streaming to Telegram or change default Telegram access policy.

  - `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.ack-sticker.test.ts`
  - `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Telegram resolves ackSticker config, sends the configured sticker before dispatching the agent turn, and optionally removes it after reply.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers config resolution, channel feedback shape, Telegram send behavior, and dispatch cleanup without requiring live Telegram credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing ack reaction tests covered reaction behavior only.
- If no new test is added, why not: New tests are added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Telegram users can configure `ackSticker` globally, per account, per direct/group chat, or per topic. When enabled, OpenClaw sends a Telegram sticker acknowledgement before generating the response. Existing ack reactions continue to work.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Telegram message -> OpenClaw agent loop -> Telegram final reply

After:
Telegram message -> ack sticker -> OpenClaw agent loop -> Telegram final reply -> optional ack sticker cleanup
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

- Runtime/container: Node 22, pnpm

```json5
{

        scope: "direct",
        silent: true,
        removeAfterReply: true,
      },
    },
  },
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure Telegram `ackSticker.fileId` with a sendable Telegram sticker file id.
2. Start the OpenClaw gateway with Telegram enabled.
3. Send a DM to the Telegram bot.

### Expected

- Telegram sends the configured acknowledgement sticker before the agent response.
- The agent response is delivered normally.
- If `removeAfterReply` is enabled, the acknowledgement sticker is deleted after the final reply.

### Actual

- Unit and config tests pass for the implemented behavior.
- Live Telegram verification was attempted locally but blocked by local gateway token/env setup issues, not by the ackSticker implementation.

## Evidence

## Human Verification (required)

  - Channel feedback carries ackSticker configuration.
  - Telegram send path sends stickers and respects notification options.
  - Telegram dispatch path can clean up ack stickers after reply.

- Edge cases checked:
  - Scope resolution mirrors ack reaction behavior.
  - Sticker cleanup is best-effort.
  - Ack reaction remains available and can be disabled independently.
- What you did **not** verify:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Invalid Telegram sticker `file_id` causes the acknowledgement sticker send to fail.
  - Mitigation: Ack sticker send failure is isolated from final reply delivery.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): add replyToOfflineMessages config to process missed messages after reconnect
target.body:
## Problem

Messages received during WhatsApp disconnects are **silently lost**. When the gateway reconnects, offline messages arrive with `upsert.type === "append"` (history sync) and are **skipped** by default.

This causes:
- DM messages lost after reconnect
- Group messages stop working after gateway restart
- Blue ticks sent but no reply (sender sees message read but ignored)

**15+ issues** trace back to the same `append` skip.

## Solution

Add opt-in config to process offline messages:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    whatsapp: {
      replyToOfflineMessages: true,
      offlineMessageMaxAgeSeconds: 300, // default: 300 (5 minutes)
    },
  },
}
```

- `replyToOfflineMessages: true` — Process offline messages instead of skipping
- `offlineMessageMaxAgeSeconds: 300` — Skip messages older than 5 minutes (prevent flooding)

## Related Issues

- **#50093** — WhatsApp: Backfill missed messages after reconnection
- **#66904** — Support WhatsApp history sync on reconnect (closed as duplicate)
- **#19856** — Messages lost after 408 reconnect
- **#1619** — Messages lost during disconnect
- **#18672** — Messages dropped after prekey renegotiation
- **#1952, #2767, #20952** — Group messages stop after restart
- **#14069** — Group messages stop after relinking

## Based On

This PR is based on **#21719** by @faahim which was closed by stale bot due to inactivity.

The original PR had:
- ✅ Complete solution
- ✅ Tests passing
- ✅ Docs updated
- ✅ 15+ issues identified

## Changes

1. `src/config/types.whatsapp.ts` — Add config fields
2. `extensions/whatsapp/src/accounts.ts` — Add resolved fields
3. `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/monitor.ts` — Implement logic
4. `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor.ts` — Pass options

## Default Behavior

**Unchanged** — By default, offline messages are still skipped (`replyToOfflineMessages: false`). Users must opt-in to process them.

---

**Context:** OpenClaw WhatsApp bundled user since 2026.3.13, currently on latest version.

target.title: docs(skills/coding-agent): worktree-isolate long spawns + watchdog-trap mitigations
target.body:
## Summary

Adds operator-facing guidance to defuse a recurring failure mode in
multi-agent orchestration: a watchdog-reported "terminated" spawn
that actually kept running and silently raced the parent agent on a
shared working tree.

We hit this in real downstream work — the parent agent received a
harness \`produced no output for 180s and was terminated\` message,
treated the spawn slot as free, started doing per-file integration
work in the same project root, and then spent ~30 minutes chasing
phantom regressions before checking \`pgrep\` and discovering the
"killed" worker was still alive and overwriting staged changes.

This PR is the operator-side workaround. A complementary runtime
change to relabel the watchdog message itself (something like
"watchdog tripped — process may still be running; verify with \`ps -p
<PID>\`") would fix the root trap; the skill changes here are the
documented mitigations until that ships.

## Changes (all in \`skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md\`)

1. **Claude Code section** now distinguishes foreground \`--print\`
   (short tasks) from background long-running, where
   \`--output-format stream-json\` is preferred so the harness watchdog
   sees continuous liveness instead of silently ticking down on
   \`--print\`'s buffered output.

2. **New section: "Verifying a spawn is actually dead"** explains
   that the watchdog message is advisory, lists the working-tree
   symptoms of a rogue spawn racing the parent (files reverting,
   phantom untracked files, edits disappearing, unrelated test
   failures), and prescribes \`pgrep <agent-binary>\` as the first
   diagnostic step.

3. **Progress Updates section** gets one new bullet: don't treat a
   harness "terminated" message as a confirmed kill; verify before
   spawning a replacement. Points readers at the new section.

4. **Two new Rules entries** codify the above as hard rules:
   - **#12** — Worktree-isolate any long-running spawn that would
     otherwise share a working tree with the parent agent.
   - **#13** — Watchdog "terminated" messages are advisory, not
     authoritative. Verify with \`ps -p\` / \`pgrep\`. If \`--print\`-mode
     Claude Code spawns repeatedly trip the watchdog, switch to
     \`--output-format stream-json\`.

## Why these specifically

- \`--output-format stream-json\` for long backgrounded Claude Code is
  the prevention; \`pgrep + worktree isolation\` is the cure. Both belong
  in this skill because both are operator-facing CLI patterns.
- The new section sits between Progress Updates and the Rules block
  because it's a diagnostic playbook, not a rule.
- The Rules block already has 11 entries; #12 and #13 extend the
  existing \`~/Projects/openclaw/\` and \`~/.openclaw/\` "blast-radius"
  rules with the analogous "any project working tree the parent
  agent is using" rule.

## Test plan

- [ ] Read the diff (62 lines added, 1 deleted; one file).
- [ ] Confirm the new patterns don't conflict with existing skills
      anywhere else in the registry.
- [ ] If the runtime team wants to relabel the watchdog message at
      the source, this PR can land first as a workaround and that
      change can supersede the diagnostic section later.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: Doctor: expose auth profile findings
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Doctor has a mature auth-profile health path, but its findings were not available through the structured lint surface. That left profile expiry, missing credentials, cooldown/disabled state, and legacy Codex provider overrides outside the `doctor --lint --only/--all` workflow.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds an opt-in structured health check, `core/doctor/auth-profiles`, wired through the existing `doctor:auth-profiles` contribution. Detection is read-only and mirrors existing auth-profile health behavior; the existing mutating repair flow remains in `runAuthProfileHealth` and the legacy repair helpers.

The check is `defaultEnabled: false`, so it is not part of default Doctor lint. It is discoverable through explicit lint selection such as `--only core/doctor/auth-profiles` and `--all`.

After ClawSweeper's P2 path finding, structured auth-profile findings now report the canonical SQLite auth store display path from `resolveAuthStorePathForDisplay(...)`, not the legacy `auth-profiles.json` path.

## User Impact

- No public SDK/plugin/config contract changes.
- Default `doctor --lint` behavior is unchanged for auth profiles.
- Users who explicitly opt in can now get structured findings for auth-profile cooldown, disabled state, expiry, missing credentials, and legacy Codex override shadowing.
- Auth-profile findings point at the current `openclaw-agent.sqlite` auth store path.
- Keychain prompts are disabled for structured detection.

## Evidence

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-auth.profile-health.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.hints.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

target.title: fix(gateway): render codex inline media once
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-harness replies that emitted local `MEDIA:` image directives could fail to become trusted outgoing media, leaving inline images text-only or unavailable to WebChat/Control UI.
- Why it matters: Agents using the Codex harness should be able to return generated/local images inline without requiring channel-specific manual workarounds, and users should not see duplicate assistant turns for one image reply.
- What changed: Trust structured local media markers from non-external tool results, while preserving the existing registered-tool trust checks; replace the latest raw assistant `MEDIA:` transcript turn with the normalized media-bearing message instead of appending a second assistant image turn.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not change external MCP tool trust, local media root serving checks, model prompting, ComfyUI plugin behavior, or any provider/network API.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

No matching upstream issue was found. Searches checked: `inline image`, `inline media`, `Codex harness image`, `MEDIA: image`, `assistant-media`, `managed media`, `Control UI image`, and duplicate image/WebChat terms.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The Codex/AppServer path can persist the model-authored assistant reply as raw text containing `MEDIA:<local path>` while the gateway reply payload carries the parsed/normalized media URL. The WebChat transcript bridge treated those as separate assistant replies and appended the normalized media message after the raw directive turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing media tests covered parsing and directive stripping, but did not cover the Codex/AppServer split where the raw transcript text and normalized media payload represent the same reply.
- Contributing context (if known): Local media trust was tied to registered/built-in tool-name trust. Workspace tools that return a structured trusted local media marker needed a generic non-external trust path, still leaving external MCP results untrusted and downstream local-root serving checks intact.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Non-external structured `trustedLocalMedia` tool results keep local media URLs, while external tool results with the same marker do not.
  - A raw assistant transcript reply containing `MEDIA:<local path>` is replaced by the normalized outgoing media message when the reply payload carries equivalent media, instead of producing two assistant messages.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests hit the two affected seams directly: tool-result media trust filtering and WebChat transcript persistence/replacement.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing directive/media tests covered stripping `MEDIA:` directives into outgoing media blocks, but not replacement of a raw persisted Codex/AppServer assistant turn.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A; new regression coverage is included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-harness agents can render trusted local image replies inline in WebChat/Control UI without leaving a raw `MEDIA:` line behind or showing a duplicate text-plus-image assistant reply.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text]
                              -> [gateway normalized media reply appended]
                              -> [duplicate assistant turns]

After:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text detected]
                              -> [latest assistant turn replaced with normalized media blocks]
                              -> [single inline image reply]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

Security notes:

- Structured local media trust is ignored for external MCP tool results.
- Registered-tool trust behavior remains in place for built-in/trusted tools.
- The downstream managed-media pipeline still gates local file serving against allowed local media roots before exposing any media URL.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local development workspace; Windows gateway hot-patch was separately smoke-tested before PR preparation.
- Runtime/container: Node local workspace.
- Model/provider: Codex harness path observed with `gpt-5.5` during manual smoke testing.
- Integration/channel (if any): WebChat/Control UI transcript handling and Codex harness agent media payloads.
- Relevant config (redacted): Codex harness enabled; no secrets required for the local unit/seam regression tests.

### Steps

1. Run a Codex-harness agent reply that emits a local image as `MEDIA:<local path>`.
2. Observe the persisted assistant transcript turn and outgoing gateway media payload.
3. Open WebChat/Control UI for the same session.

### Expected

- The reply appears once as an inline image-bearing assistant message.
- The raw `MEDIA:` directive is not shown to the user.
- Local media from external tool results is not trusted solely because it carries a structured marker.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the raw `MEDIA:` assistant text could remain in the transcript and the normalized media message could be appended as a second assistant reply.
- Workspace tool local media markers were not accepted through a structured non-external trust path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Passing local verification on the rebased branch:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
# passed
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Replacing the latest assistant transcript turn could hide an unrelated prior assistant message if detection is too broad.
  - Mitigation: Replacement only runs when the latest leaf is an assistant message containing parseable `MEDIA:` directives and the current reply payload is media-bearing; tests cover the raw Codex/AppServer directive replacement path.
- Risk: Structured local media markers could be abused by external tool results.
  - Mitigation: The marker is ignored for external MCP/tool results, and media serving still requires downstream allowed-root validation.

## Real behavior proof

Current head tested: fede70512ea83677a9c79bd7530bf6865818125f.

target.title: Add provider-aware automatic TTS emotion mapping
target.body:
## Summary
- Add opt-in `messages.tts.autoEmotion` support that infers a conservative abstract emotion from synthesized text.
- Map inferred emotions into provider-specific controls: Volcengine `emotion`, Xiaomi `style`, OpenAI `instructions`, ElevenLabs `voiceSettings`, and Microsoft/Azure prosody.
- Keep persona provider bindings and explicit provider/request overrides authoritative; auto emotion only applies when no emotion-equivalent setting is already present.
- Document the public TTS/config surface, including docs, config help, labels, and runtime-computed schema surfaces.
- Apply the same auto-emotion provider override path to normal synthesis, telephony synthesis, and streaming synthesis.

## Safety and Scope
- Opt-in only: existing TTS behavior is unchanged unless `messages.tts.autoEmotion.enabled` is set.
- Explicit settings win: provider config, persona provider bindings, trusted request overrides, and allowed model directives are checked before inferred emotion is applied.
- Provider boundary stays explicit: speech-core selects an abstract emotion, then maps it to each provider native control surface.
- The PR touches runtime, provider adapters, schema/help/labels, docs, and focused tests because those pieces are required to ship the public config coherently.
- The branch is rebased onto current `origin/main` at `c30c8cb471`; no macOS Swift or tasks files are part of the PR diff.

## Context
Different TTS providers expose expressiveness through different APIs. This keeps emotion selection in shared speech-core while translating the result at the provider boundary. Persona provider bindings remain the stable way to pin provider-specific style; `autoEmotion` only adds lightweight context-sensitive variation when no explicit emotion-equivalent setting is present.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Opt-in `messages.tts.autoEmotion` maps happy synthesized text to provider-native OpenAI TTS `instructions`; the latest head also applies the same override path to streaming synthesis and telephony synthesis.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw CLI from this PR branch at commit `473f313469`, using an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, with the real OpenAI TTS provider wired to a local OpenAI-compatible HTTP capture endpoint. Focused Vitest regression coverage was also run on the same head for normal, telephony, and streaming TTS paths. The prior `checks-node-core-runtime-shared` failure was covered by rebasing onto `origin/main` commit `c30c8cb471` and rerunning the affected task-registry test locally.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_HOME=$TMP/home \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=$TMP/openclaw.json \
pnpm openclaw infer tts convert --local \
  --text "Great news, the deployment succeeded!" \
  --output $TMP/out.mp3 \
  --json

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts

git diff --check
codex review --commit HEAD
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
{
  "ok": true,
  "capability": "tts.convert",
  "transport": "local",
  "provider": "openai",
  "attempts": [
    {
      "provider": "openai",
      "outcome": "success",
      "reasonCode": "success",
      "personaBinding": "none",
      "latencyMs": 276
    }
  ],
  "outputs": [
    {
      "path": "$TMP/out.mp3",
      "format": "mp3",
      "voiceCompatible": false
    }
  ]
}

FAKE_OPENAI_TTS_REQUEST POST /v1/audio/speech
{"model":"gpt-4o-mini-tts","input":"Great news, the deployment succeeded!","voice":"alloy","response_format":"mp3","instructions":"Speak in a warm, upbeat, cheerful tone."}

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       42 passed (42)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       67 passed (67)

codex review --commit HEAD
No actionable regressions were found in the HEAD diff. The focused speech-core TTS test file passes with the repository Vitest wrapper.
```

Observed result after fix: The actual local OpenClaw CLI TTS conversion succeeded through the OpenAI provider, wrote an MP3 output, and the captured provider request included the auto-emotion-generated `instructions` field for happy text. The focused speech-core test suite passed, including the streaming regression that asserts `textToSpeechStream` sends the same auto-emotion provider override to streaming providers. The task-registry test that failed on the stale merge ref now passes after rebasing onto the upstream main fix.

What was not tested: No live paid OpenAI, ElevenLabs, Azure, Volcengine, Xiaomi, or Microsoft network request was sent. Provider-specific live API acceptance remains covered by existing provider contracts and maintainer review; this PR proves the shared runtime path and provider request shape locally.

## Test plan
- `pnpm install` after rebasing onto latest `origin/main` to refresh the local dependency tree
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_HOME=$TMP/home OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=$TMP/openclaw.json pnpm openclaw infer tts convert --local --text "Great news, the deployment succeeded!" --output $TMP/out.mp3 --json`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --commit HEAD`

target.title: fix(googlechat): preserve unresolved service account refs
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve unresolved Google Chat `serviceAccountRef` as an error instead of silently falling back to legacy inline `serviceAccount` credentials.
- Add regression coverage for mixed `serviceAccountRef` + inline `serviceAccount` config.

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Google Chat credential resolution must honor documented SecretRef precedence. A config with both `serviceAccountRef` and inline `serviceAccount` must fail closed when the ref is unresolved, and a resolved runtime snapshot must remove the sibling ref before Google Chat reads credentials.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR worktree on the rebased head `ef2bfcd48f`, using Node/pnpm from the repo environment. I checked env and `~/.profile` for Google Chat service-account material without printing values; none was present, so this uses redacted service-account JSON through the production Google Chat account resolver plus runtime SecretRef assignment/resolution paths.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `pnpm exec tsx` against `extensions/googlechat/src/accounts.ts` and `extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.ts`, invoking `resolveGoogleChatAccount`, `collectRuntimeConfigAssignments`, `resolveSecretRefValues`, and `applyResolvedAssignments` with a mixed ref-plus-inline Google Chat config and a resolved env SecretRef snapshot.

**Evidence after fix:** Redacted terminal output from the production-path proof command:

```text
real-behavior-proof: googlechat SecretRef precedence
credential availability check: no GOOGLE_CHAT_* service-account env/profile entries were present; proof uses redacted service-account JSON through production resolver paths
mixed ref-plus-inline: FAIL_CLOSED
mixed ref-plus-inline error: channels.googlechat.accounts.default.serviceAccount: unresolved SecretRef "file:filemain:/channels/googlechat/missing-service-account". Resolve this command against an active gateway runtime snapshot before reading it.
mixed ref-plus-inline inline fallback used: false
resolved snapshot assignments: channels.googlechat.accounts.prod.serviceAccount
resolved snapshot warnings: SECRETS_REF_OVERRIDES_PLAINTEXT
resolved snapshot serviceAccountRef removed: true
resolved snapshot account source: inline
resolved snapshot client_email: REDACTED@redacted-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
resolved snapshot private_key: REDACTED
resolved snapshot inline legacy used: false
```

**Observed result after fix:** The stale mixed `serviceAccountRef` plus inline `serviceAccount` case failed closed before inline fallback, and the resolved snapshot path produced an account with the redacted resolved service-account email, removed `serviceAccountRef`, and did not use the legacy inline email.

**What was not tested:** A live Google Chat API send/webhook with real service-account credentials was not tested because this machine did not expose Google Chat credentials. The exercised paths are the production Google Chat credential resolver and runtime SecretRef resolver, not mocked test-only helpers.

## Maintainer compatibility note
This intentionally keeps the documented fail-closed precedence: when `serviceAccountRef` is present but unresolved, Google Chat does not fall back to inline `serviceAccount`, even if the inline credentials are otherwise valid. Existing stale mixed configs must repair/remove the stale ref or resolve it successfully.

## Tests
- `pnpm test extensions/googlechat/src/setup.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/googlechat/src/accounts.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.ts extensions/googlechat/src/setup.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json`
- `pnpm check:changed`
